description_long: This is a finely detailed wooden sculpture depicting the Hindu deity Ganesha, characterized by an elephant head with a long, curved trunk and large, ornate ears. The figure is seated cross-legged on a round, intricately carved pedestal base, with one hand raised in a blessing gesture and another holding a modak (sweet). The sculpture features elaborate carvings on the headdress, trunk, and body, with visible jewelry and decorative patterns throughout. The back of the sculpture shows the detailed headdress and the folds of the garment, while the sides reveal the depth of the carving and the three-dimensionality of the arms and trunk. The surface has a matte, slightly rough finish typical of hand-carved wood, and the color is a warm, natural wood tone. This type of sculpture is commonly found in public spaces, temples, or as a decorative element in urban parks and plazas.
description: A hand-carved wooden sculpture of the Hindu deity Ganesha, seated on a round pedestal with intricate detailing and a natural wood finish.
description_view_0: Front view showing Ganesha's face, trunk, raised hand, and detailed chest and pedestal carvings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the trunk, arm, and the layered carving of the headdress and body.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the headdress, shoulders, and the draped garment details.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the trunk's curve, arm, and the depth of the carving on the side of the body and base.
attribute_subject_matter: Ganesha, the Hindu elephant-headed deity, seated in a traditional pose
attribute_location_context: No specific installation context is visible; the object could be placed in a public park, urban plaza, or temple setting.
